study.journal: The New England Journal of Medicine. 2019;380(21):1997-2008.

population_context.population_description: Patients with moderate-to-severe ARDS defined by a ratio of partial pressure of arterial oxygen to fraction of inspired oxygen of less than 150 mm Hg with positive end-expiratory pressure of at least 8 cm of water, undergoing mechanical ventilation through an endotracheal tube, enrolled early after onset (median 7.6 hours).
population_context.risk_group: Moderate-to-severe ARDS patients on mechanical ventilation

intervention.name: Early continuous neuromuscular blockade with deep sedation
components: Intravenous bolus of 15 mg cisatracurium followed by continuous infusion
intervention.parameter_values: 15 mg intravenous bolus of cisatracurium, then 37.5 mg per hour continuous infusion for 48 hours; deep sedation targets (Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale −2 to −3, Riker 1-2, or Ramsay 4-6); low tidal volume ventilation within 2 hours after randomization; high PEEP strategy for up to 5 days
intervention.timing: Within 4 hours after randomization in patients not already under deep sedation; started early after ARDS onset (median 7.6 hours)

comparator.description: Usual care without routine neuromuscular blockade and with lighter sedation targets (Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale 0 to −1, Riker 3-4, or Ramsay 2-3); same mechanical ventilation strategies including low tidal volume and high PEEP

outcome_effect.outcome_name: In-hospital death from any cause at 90 days

strengths: Prospective multicenter randomized controlled trial design
Pre-specified statistical analysis plan published before data analysis
Blinded outcome assessors at 3, 6, and 12 months
Standardized mechanical ventilation protocols in both groups
Central institutional review board and data safety monitoring board oversight
limitations: Trial stopped at second interim analysis for futility, potentially reducing statistical power
Unblinded study design may introduce bias
Lighter sedation targets in control group differ from deep sedation in ACURASYS trial, limiting comparability
Neuromuscular blockade dosing not titrated to peripheral nerve stimulation in intervention group
Limited long-term neuromuscular outcome data collection
adverse_events: Patients in intervention group were less physically active while in hospital
More adverse cardiovascular events in intervention group compared to control group
Increased recall of paralysis in intervention group (assessed with modified Brice questionnaire)

applicability.generalizability_notes: Findings apply to patients with moderate-to-severe ARDS on mechanical ventilation treated with high PEEP strategies. Results may not generalize to milder ARDS, non-ventilated patients, or settings using different ventilation approaches. Findings represent current practice with lighter sedation targets rather than the deep sedation strategy of prior ACURASYS trial.

quoted_sentences.design: We designed the ROSE trial to be consistent with certain elements of the ACURASYS trial. Similarities included the use of the same neuromuscular blocking agent (cisatracurium) with the same dosing regimen and duration of treatment. A key difference was our use of lighter sedation targets in the control group to be consistent with current practice recommendations.
quoted_sentences.intervention: We randomly assigned patients in a 1:1 ratio to receive 48 hours of continuous neuromuscular blockade with concomitant deep sedation (intervention group) or to receive usual care without routine neuromuscular blockade and with lighter sedation targets (control group). Patients in the intervention group who were not under deep sedation at baseline were deeply sedated within 4 hours after randomization. Subsequently, patients in this group received an intravenous bolus of 15 mg of cisatracurium, followed by a continuous infusion of 37.5 mg per hour for 48 hours.
quoted_sentences.results: At 90 days, 213 patients (42.5%) in the intervention group and 216 (42.8%) in the control group had died before hospital discharge (between-group difference, −0.3 percentage points; 95% confidence interval, −6.4 to 5.9; P = 0.93).
quoted_sentences.outcome: Among patients with moderate-to-severe ARDS who were treated with a strategy involving a high PEEP, there was no significant difference in mortality at 90 days between patients who received an early and continuous cisatracurium infusion and those who were treated with a usual-care approach with lighter sedation targets.
